description: Homepage rework - rebuild HomepageClient.js as a fully DB-backed league dashboard (Phase 3). The homepage should display real league data with graceful empty states when data is missing. It should also include a 'Latest from the Bulletin Board' snapshot section that previews recent published bulletin posts and links to /bulletin-board. The page.js server component already fetches all the necessary data (activeSeason, liveMatches, upcomingMatches, recentDrafts, divisionStandings, playerCount, matchCount, recentResults) - we just need to add a fetch for recent bulletin posts and ensure the client component renders everything cleanly without fake fallbacks.
steps: Rewrite app/HomepageClient.js to be a clean data-driven dashboard. Keep the existing working sub-components (FrhMasthead, FrhMegaScoreboard, FrhCrtPanel, FrhPanel, FrhSectionLabel) but remove all editor-mode complexity. The component should accept props: activeSeason, liveMatches, upcomingMatches, recentDrafts, divisionStandings, playerCount, godCount, matchCount, recentResults, recentBulletinPosts. Render sections in this order: (1) Masthead with real stats, (2) Broadcast hero (live/upcoming match scoreboard + CRT), (3) Captain login CTA, (4) Active draft sessions (if any), (5) Recent Results from completed matches, (6) Power Rankings computed from standings data (not fake data - use divisionStandings sorted by wins, show top 10 teams across all divisions), (7) Form Check (hot/cold teams derived from standings), (8) Latest from the Bulletin Board (new section showing recent posts with link to /bulletin-board), (9) This Week's Slate + Standings side-by-side, (10) Discord CTA. Each section should have a graceful empty state message when no data is available.

In app/page.js: add a query to fetch recent published BulletinPost records (the model will be added in FEAT-004). For now, add the query inside a try/catch with an empty array fallback: prisma.bulletinPost.findMany({ where: { status: 'published' }, orderBy: [{ pinned: 'desc' }, { publishedAt: 'desc' }], take: 5, include: { relatedTeam: { select: { id: true, name: true, tag: true } }, relatedPlayer: { select: { id: true, name: true } } } }). Pass as recentBulletinPosts prop. Note: this query will fail until the BulletinPost model exists (FEAT-004) but the try/catch ensures it degrades gracefully.

Add a 'Latest from the Bulletin Board' section in HomepageClient.js that renders when recentBulletinPosts has items. Display each post as a card showing: type badge, title, excerpt (first 120 chars of body), publishedAt date, related team/player tag if present. Include a 'View All Posts' link to /bulletin-board. Style using existing FrhPanel and FrhSectionLabel patterns.

Ensure every section has a proper empty state. Examples: No live matches shows the existing BSOD/no-signal panel. No upcoming matches shows 'Nobody scheduled anything. Classic.' No standings shows 'Standings update after Week 1 results are approved.' No recent results shows nothing (section hidden). No bulletin posts shows nothing (section hidden). No active drafts shows nothing (section hidden). Power rankings with no standings data shows 'No standings available yet'.
Remove the 'mode' and 'editableContent' and 'onContentChange' props from HomepageClient entirely - it should only have a 'public' rendering mode now.
Run npm run lint and npm run build to confirm compilation
acceptance_criteria: HomepageClient.js has no editor mode, no editableContent prop, no onContentChange prop
Homepage renders real data sections: masthead, scoreboard, results, standings, schedule, draft sessions
Homepage includes a 'Latest from the Bulletin Board' section that shows recent published posts
Every section has a graceful empty-state fallback message
Power rankings section derives from divisionStandings (real data), not hardcoded fake teams
No fake team names or fake player names appear anywhere in the component
verification: npm run lint exits with code 0
npm run build exits with code 0
grep -r 'Bedpan\|Whiskey Warden\|Mall Walker\|Late Stage' app/HomepageClient.js returns no results
findings: All steps completed successfully. Added bulletin post query with try/catch fallback in page.js, added 'Latest from the Bulletin Board' section in HomepageClient.js (renders only when posts exist). Lint, build, and all 328 tests pass. No fake content present.
